mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real estate financing (RSAFS) signals strong household sector confidence, driving equity valuations through improved consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 60%+ of periods when RSAFS grows by >2% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates accommodative monetary policy, reducing discount rates for equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 50%+ of instances when FEDFUNDS declines by >0.15pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 9.0% signals improving labor market conditions, boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 40%+ of periods when UNRATE declines by >0.2pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 93.5 indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 50%+ of instances when INDPRO rises by >0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signal deteriorating economic demand, creating headwinds for corporate earnings and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances when both GDPC1 and HOUST decline by >1% quarter-over-quarter.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% combined with stable interest rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates persistent price pressures, prompting central bank hawkishness and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in 50%+ of instances when CPIAUCSL > 2.5 and FEDFUNDS < 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 130,000 jobs combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals labor market overheating, creating inflationary pressures that drive negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 40%+ of instances when PAYEMS > 130000 and UNRATE > 9.0.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 540 thousand units indicates weakening consumer demand for durable goods, creating headwinds for corporate revenues and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods when HOUST declines by >1% month-over-month.
